strand_description: The ABM strand develops students with foundational knowledge in business, finance, marketing, and organizational management. It prepares students for careers in the business sector and equips them with practical skills in accounting, economics, and entrepreneurship.
core_academic_subjects: Fundamentals of Accountancy, Business, and Management 1 and 2
holland_rationale: ABM students are predominantly Enterprising due to the business and leadership focus of their curriculum. Conventional is common because accounting and finance subjects require precision, rule-following, and systematic data management. Social emerges in students drawn to marketing, HR, and management roles.
grade_note: ABM graduates with very high GWA (90+) and strong math performance are excellent BSA candidates. Students with average GWA (85–89) and strong communication or leadership skills are better suited for BSBA or BSHRM.
common_student_profiles: The Future Accountant — loves numbers, detail-oriented, disciplined, Conventional/Enterprising type
The Entrepreneur — business-minded, risk-tolerant, leadership-driven, Enterprising type
The Marketing Student — creative in business contexts, people-oriented, Enterprising/Social type
The HR Track Student — interested in people and organizations, Social/Enterprising type
